title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Apr 07, 2026
summary: US equity markets continued a sharp tariff-driven selloff on April 7, with the S&P 500 off roughly 10% from recent highs and JPMorgan CEO Dimon warning of rising recession probability — days after Trump shared a video framing the market decline as intentional. The Artemis II crew exited the lunar sphere of influence heading home after a record-breaking flyby, setting a new crewed distance mark at 252,760 miles from Earth and targeting Pacific splashdown around April 11. An ABC News review found Trump's legal team citing the 2024 SCOTUS presidential immunity ruling in nearly one-third of second-term emergency SCOTUS filings, spanning federal firings, immigration, and trade authority. NBC News also reported that US Marshals waived training requirements to deputize Musk's private security as armed federal agents during DOGE's government access phase.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: Artemis II completed a historic lunar flyby on April 6 — the first humans near the Moon since Apollo 17 in 1972 — as restructured 50% Section 232 tariffs on steel, aluminum, and copper took effect simultaneously. Trump also imposed up to 100% tariffs on patented pharmaceutical imports (signed April 2), signed an executive order on voter citizenship verification (March 31), and the administration surpassed 2.5 million total immigration departures with ICE detention at a record ~68,000. DOGE's one-year mark drew competing analyses: $160B in claimed savings versus $135B in estimated disruption costs.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: DOGE One-Year Assessment: $160B Claimed Savings vs. $135B Estimated Disruption Costs.

summary: Trump Shares Video Claiming He Is Deliberately Crashing the Market to Force Fed Rate Cuts. DHS published an enforcement update on April 4 highlighting ICE arrests of gang members, sex offenders, and stalkers as part of ongoing interior enforcement operations.
